key_insight: AdsMind adds a physics-grounded feedback loop to LLM-driven adsorption search, letting the system correct bad guesses after MLFF relaxation instead of relying on a single open-loop proposal.
engineering_relevance: This can make heterogeneous catalyst screening faster and more reliable by sharply reducing the number of relaxations needed to find low-energy adsorbate configurations.
method: Multi-agent LLM framework for proposing adsorption configurations
MLFF relaxation feedback used to detect and correct errors in a closed loop
Cross-backend self-correction to improve robustness and reduce energy dispersion
evidence_signal: Across AA20 and OCD-GMAE62, AdsMind achieved 100% and 98.8% success rates, with only 4.11 and 4.67 MLFF relaxations per case, and DFT checks on six AA20 systems showed correct adsorption-energy sign in all tested cases.
watch_item: Performance is benchmark-dependent and still relies on MLFF and DFT validation quality, so transfer to new surface-adsorbate chemistries may require careful retuning and verification.
paper_title: AdsMind: A Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent System for Self-Correcting Discovery of Adsorption Configurations on Heterogeneous Catalyst Surfaces
